title: D.C. Code § 1-1001.01 — Election of electors.
text:
Election of electors.
In the District of Columbia electors of President and Vice President of the United States, the Delegate to the House of Representatives, the members of the State Board of Education, the members of the Council of the District of Columbia, the Attorney General for the District of Columbia, the Mayor and the following officials of political parties in the District of Columbia shall be elected as provided in this subchapter:
(1) National committeemen and national committeewomen;
(2) Delegates to conventions and conferences of political parties including delegates to nominate candidates for the Presidency and Vice Presidency of the United States;
(3) Alternates to the officials referred to in paragraphs (1) and (2) of this section, where permitted by political party rules; and
(4) Such members and officials of local committees of political parties as may be designated by the duly authorized local committees of such parties for election at large or by ward in the District of Columbia.

--- Annotations ---
[History] Aug. 12, 1955, 69 Stat. 699, ch. 862, § 1
[History] Oct. 4, 1961, 75 Stat. 817, Pub. L. 87-389, § 1(1)
[History] Apr. 22, 1968, 82 Stat. 103, Pub. L. 90-292, § 4(1)
[History] Sept. 22, 1970, 84 Stat. 853, Pub. L. 91-405, title II, § 205(e)(1)
[History] Dec. 23, 1971, 85 Stat. 788, Pub. L. 92-220, § 1(1)
[History] Dec. 24, 1973, 87 Stat. 832, Pub. L. 93-198, title VII, § 751(1)
[History] Aug. 18, 1978, D.C. Law 2-101, § 2, 25 DCR 257
[History] Mar. 16, 1982, D.C. Law 4-88, § 2(a), 29 DCR 458
[History] Oct. 18, 2007, D.C. Law 17-26, § 2(a), 54 DCR 8018
[History] May 27, 2010, D.C. Law 18-160, § 131(a), 57 DCR 3012
[History] Mar. 3, 2015, D.C. Law 20-167, § 2(a), 61 DCR 10738
[Emergency Legislation] For temporary (90 days) amendment of this section, see § 2(a) of the Special Election Reform Emergency Amendment Act of 2014 (D.C. Act 20-617, Jan. 28, 2015,  62 DCR 1908, 21 STAT 794).
[Effect of Amendments] The 2015 amendment by D.C. Law 20-167 substituted “State Board of Education” for “Board of Education” in the introductory paragraph.
[Effect of Amendments] D.C. Law 18-160, in the introductory language, inserted “the Attorney General for the District of Columbia,”.
[Effect of Amendments] D.C. Law 17-26, in par. (2), deleted “: Provided, that all elections for delegates to conventions and conferences of political parties, upon the request of the said party, shall be scheduled at the same time as primary, general, or special elections already scheduled for other purposes” following “United States”.
[Prior Codifications] 1973 Ed., § 1-1101.
[Prior Codifications] 1981 Ed., § 1-1301.
[Section References] This section is referenced in § 1-1001.08, § 1-1001.10, § 1-1161.01, § 1-1163.02, § 1-1163.05, § 1-1163.06, § 1-1163.16, § 1-1163.35, and § 38-2651.
[Cross References] Statehood constitutional convention initiative, delegates, at-large and ward candidates, see § 1-124.
[Cross References] House of representatives, delegates, privileges and restrictions, qualifications, see § 1-401.
[Cross References] Elections, “political party” defined, see § 1-1101.01.
[Cross References] Council, elections, terms of office, vacancies, see § 1-204.01.
[Cross References] Board of Elections and Ethics, fines and penalties, advisory opinions, see § 1-1103.05.
[Cross References] Advisory neighborhood commissions, elections, authorization, see § 1-309.09.
